title: Night Meadow
tileHint: A meadow after dark, where thin shelter, prowling shapes, and restless sleep shape the encounter

1.hint: Keep still, listen to the dark, and let the meadow tell you what is truly moving there.
1.successTitle: A quiet watcher keeps your sleep
1.successBody: $companionName stays by your road as though it had already chosen you while you lay under the tree.
1.failureBody: You weather the darkness safely, but the hidden life of the meadow keeps its distance.

2.hint: Offer warmth or mercy in the dark without claiming what answers you.
2.successBody: $companionName remains with you, drawn by the gentleness you kept even in fear and cold.
2.failureBody: You help what you can, yet the creature chooses the dark meadow over your company.

3.hint: Stand up to the roaming danger of the night and make a place of safety where none was certain.
3.successTitle: A fierce ally accepts your fire
3.successBody: $companionName stays after the danger passes, as though boldness has earned a place near it.
3.failureTitle: The night is broken, but no bond remains
3.failureBody: Your courage changes the shape of the night, though the wild thing you saved keeps its own freedom.

startText: Night on the meadow is colder than it first appears. The grass whispers in every small wind, shadows move low and uncertain, and even sleep feels like a bargain made with whatever roams beyond the tree line.

start.text: You make a little resting place under a tree at the edge of the meadow. What finds you there depends on how you meet the dark.

choices.label: Lie still and listen before acting
Keep gentleness even after the cold and fear have settled in
Rise at once when danger circles the shelter

watch1.text: You remain still beneath the tree and let the meadow's night sounds sort themselves into true meaning: grass-mice, wind-shift, distant paws, and one quieter thing that does not move like a hunter.

choices.label: Follow the soft step that pauses between the shadows
Wait until the hidden creature comes close of its own will

kind1.text: The night is long enough that even the smallest act of mercy matters. Some creatures do not need mastery from you, only a little shelter from cold, fear, or hunger.

choices.label: Share warmth under the tree roots
Offer a little food without demanding trust in return

bold1.text: Night meadow danger is not always a single beast. Sometimes it is a hungry ring of eyes, the confidence of prowlers, and the knowledge that something weaker than you is already caught between them.

choices.label: Drive the prowlers back before they close in
Stand over the trapped creature and refuse the night its meal

final.text: By the time the eastern sky begins to pale, the meadow has decided what kind of company, if any, your night was worthy to keep.

commitChoices.label: Listen first and let the hidden watcher reveal itself
Offer warmth or mercy beneath the tree
Stand up to what prowls the meadow in the dark
